headline: Claude Code adoption fractures as users report cancellations and limitations
body: Multiple developers are publicly canceling Claude Code subscriptions while discussing the tool's constraints, according to coverage across Hacker News and industry outlets. The churn signals user friction in AI-assisted coding, a capability healthcare organizations increasingly rely on for clinical workflow automation and EHR customization. For healthcare IT consulting practices, this volatility in Claude's market position raises questions about long-term viability when recommending AI coding tools for high-stakes healthcare implementations. The competing emergence of alternatives like Kimi K3 and desktop solutions further fragments the vendor landscape, forcing healthcare organizations to reassess their AI coding infrastructure strategy before deeper integration into clinical systems.
